### Post by alexv305 on 2007-12-26
How do I fix Frostwire? It constantly says "starting connection". I have no firewall and my router is set to DMZ just for now to see if I could get it to work, but still nothing. I also searched and got many results with fixes but none of them work. I already tried editing the gnutella.net file or whatever it is..

Anyone know the real fix for this?

### Post by alwayshere on 2008-02-27
got something you can try 

in terminal 

sudo nautilus

now push ctrl and h buttons

hopfully you should see frostwire file (if not look around for it )

open that frostwire folder and you should see Gnutella.net file open it and delete everthing in it and copy and paste the below text intoit then save it 

uhc2.limewire.com:20181,,1162480626164,,,en,0
krill.shacknet.nu:20095,,1162480628374,,,en,1
uhc.udp-host-cache.com:9999,,1162480630012,,,en,0
kisama.ath.cx:8080,,1162480628074,,,en,1
uhc3.limewire.com:51180,,1162480626905,,,en,0
gwc.jooz.net:8010,,1162480627623,,,en,0
cache.kicks-***.net:8000,,1162480627277,,,en,1 




--------------------------------------------------------

now all should be good fire up frostwire 

-------------------------------------------------------

### Post by cbiere on 2008-02-27
The most important thing is that you keep yourself informed and up-to-date. The only functional addresses from the given list are these three:

uhc2.limewire.com:20181,,1162480626164,,,en,0
uhc.udp-host-cache.com:9999,,1162480630012,,,en,0
uhc3.limewire.com:51180,,1162480626905,,,en,0

The others have been DEAD FOR YEARS. You can see from the numeric timestamps that this list is from November from 2006. If you actually want to help people then don't pass around old outdated lists. All those hosts belong to LimeWire anyway. What if they sell out to the RIAA like EVERYONE else of the commercial vendors did?

I have offered much better advise:
[http://ubuntuforums.org/showpost.php?p=3794687&postcount=6](http://ubuntuforums.org/showpost.php?p=3794687&postcount=6)

If that's too complicated, you're using the wrong software or Gnutella is simply the wrong tool for you. BitTorrent is almost fool-proof, Gnutella isn't.

---
